one_liner: A 41-year-old man with advanced HIV presents with two weeks of painless blurry vision and floaters in the right eye, along with new odynophagia and bloody diarrhea.

stage2.hpi_extension: He describes a curtain of dark spots floating across the right visual field and loss of peripheral vision over 10 days. He also reports burning retrosternal pain with swallowing, several watery-bloody bowel movements daily, and mild lower abdominal cramping. No eye pain, headache, photophobia, fevers, or cough. He has not taken antiretrovirals in 2 years and missed his last clinic visit.
stage2.pmh: HIV/AIDS diagnosed 9 years ago, prior PCP pneumonia, oral thrush
stage2.social_history: Lives alone. Former IV drug use, now in recovery. Non-smoker.
stage2.physical_exam: General: cachectic. HEENT: oral thrush. Funduscopy of the right eye shows fluffy yellow-white retinal exudates with scattered hemorrhages along the vessels — the classic 'pizza pie' appearance. Visual acuity 20/200 OD, 20/25 OS. Abdomen: diffuse tenderness, no peritoneal signs; stool guaiac positive. No lymphadenopathy. Skin: no rash.

lab_results.result: HIV viral load >800,000 copies/mL; CD4 count 22 cells/microL. CMV PCR in plasma markedly elevated (quantitative viral load high). CMV IgG positive (prior exposure).
lab_results.clinical_significance: CD4 <50 is the classic risk threshold for end-organ CMV disease in HIV. A high plasma CMV viral load supports reactivation and systemic disease.

lab_results.result: Colonoscopy with biopsy of colonic ulcers: large cells with enlarged nuclei and prominent eosinophilic intranuclear inclusions with a clear halo ('owl's eye' inclusions) and basophilic cytoplasmic inclusions. CMV immunohistochemistry strongly positive.
lab_results.clinical_significance: 'Owl's eye' intranuclear inclusions are pathognomonic for CMV. Tissue biopsy is the gold standard for diagnosing CMV end-organ disease (colitis, esophagitis).

lab_results.result: CT abdomen/pelvis: segmental colonic wall thickening with pericolonic stranding. No perforation or abscess. Upper endoscopy reveals large linear esophageal ulcers consistent with CMV esophagitis.
lab_results.clinical_significance: CMV colitis and esophagitis are characteristic end-organ manifestations in patients with very low CD4 counts.

stage5.final_clue: He is admitted and started on IV ganciclovir, with transition to oral valganciclovir for maintenance. Ophthalmology confirms CMV retinitis and performs an intravitreal ganciclovir injection for immediate local control. ART is re-initiated after a short delay, with counseling about immune recovery uveitis as a potential complication of immune reconstitution.

debrief.classic_presentation: CMV disease in the immunocompromised host takes several classic forms: CMV retinitis (painless floaters and progressive visual loss with 'pizza pie' retinal hemorrhages and exudates in HIV with CD4 <50), CMV colitis (bloody diarrhea, abdominal pain, ulcers on colonoscopy), CMV esophagitis (odynophagia with large linear ulcers), and CMV pneumonitis (especially in stem cell and solid organ transplant recipients). Congenital CMV is the most common congenital infection, causing sensorineural hearing loss, periventricular calcifications, microcephaly, chorioretinitis, and hepatosplenomegaly ('blueberry muffin' rash). Primary CMV infection in immunocompetent hosts causes a heterophile-negative mononucleosis-like illness.
debrief.pathophysiology: Cytomegalovirus is a double-stranded DNA herpesvirus (HHV-5) transmitted via bodily fluids, transplacentally, via transplantation, and via transfusion of non-leukoreduced blood. After primary infection, the virus establishes latency in mononuclear cells and can reactivate with immunosuppression. Infected cells show characteristic cytomegaly with intranuclear 'owl's eye' inclusions surrounded by a clear halo plus cytoplasmic inclusions. Disease reflects lytic replication in end organs and the host cell-mediated immune response against infected tissues.
debrief.treatment_overview: Ganciclovir IV is first-line for severe/end-organ disease; valganciclovir is the oral prodrug for less severe or maintenance therapy. Foscarnet is used for ganciclovir-resistant strains or when bone marrow suppression limits ganciclovir (think: foscarnet causes nephrotoxicity and electrolyte disturbances; ganciclovir causes neutropenia). Cidofovir is an alternative. For CMV retinitis, intravitreal ganciclovir injections or implants may be used. Restoration of immunity (ART in HIV, reduction of immunosuppression in transplant) is essential; watch for immune recovery uveitis.
high_yield_pearls: Owl's eye intranuclear inclusions on histology = CMV.
CMV retinitis: pizza pie fundus (hemorrhages + exudates along vessels) in HIV with CD4 <50 — painless visual loss.
CMV is the most common congenital infection; classic triad includes periventricular calcifications, sensorineural hearing loss, and chorioretinitis.
Ganciclovir causes neutropenia; foscarnet causes nephrotoxicity and electrolyte wasting (low Ca/Mg/K/PO4).
CMV causes a heterophile-negative mononucleosis syndrome that resembles EBV but lacks the atypical lymphocytes of classic mono.
sources: First Aid for the USMLE Step 1 (2017) — Microbiology chapter, 'Herpesviruses — Cytomegalovirus' section.
First Aid for the USMLE Step 1 (2017) — Microbiology chapter, 'HIV-positive adults — common disease by CD4 count' table and 'Antiviral therapy — ganciclovir/foscarnet' subsection.
